payload.title: July 2023 Financial and Ridership Reports / August 2023 Financial and Ridership Reports
payload.description: NYCT, SIR and MTA Bus financial and ridership reports covering July 2023 and August 2023 including Mid-Year Forecast performance, accrual statements, cash receipts, positions, and ridership data.

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Total revenues of $2,900.1 million were $93.8 million (3.1%) lower than the Forecast
Total ridership was 872.0 million, which was higher than the Forecast by 7.1 million (0.8%)
Total expenses of $7,412.3 million including non-cash liabilities were $137.8 million (1.8%) lower than the Forecast
total headcount was 46,622, which was 3,759 lower than the Forecast of 50,381

tool_args.label: NYCT July YTD Operating Expenses Explanation

payload.claim_text: Operating expenses under Forecast by $140.3M (2.2%) - labor costs under $126.7M (2.6%) due to vacancies, non-labor under $13.5M (1.0%) due to favorable timing of payments
payload.subject: NYCT

evidence_refs.source_quote: Operating expenses were under the Forecast by $140.3 million (2.2%) attributed to labor costs underruns of $126.7 million (2.6%) due to vacancies and non-labor expenses underruns of $13.5 million (1.0%) mainly due to favorable timing of the payments

payload.claim_text: NYCT Overtime was $113.3 million (38.2%) unfavorable primarily due to vacancy/absentee coverage needs and additional maintenance requirements
payload.subject: NYCT

evidence_refs.source_quote: Overtime was $113.3 million (38.2%) unfavorable primarily due to vacancy / absentee coverage needs and additional maintenance requirements

payload.claim_text: Paratransit Contracts unfavorable by $6.7M (2.3%) due to higher support cost charges partially offset by fewer trips
payload.subject: NYCT

evidence_refs.source_quote: Paratransit Contracts were $6.7 million (2.3%) unfavorable primarily due to higher than projected support cost charges partially offset by fewer trips

tool_args.label: NYCT July YTD Revenue per Passenger

evidence_refs.source_quote: The year-to-date July Revenue per Passenger was $2.21, which was lower than the Forecast by $0.02

payload.gap_text: Results are based on the preliminary close of the general ledger and are subject to review and adjustment
payload.missing_information: Post-close adjustments not included; will be captured in subsequent month's YTD results

evidence_refs.source_quote: Results are based on the preliminary close of the general ledger and are subject to review and adjustment. Please note actuals do not include post-close adjustments, which will be captured in the subsequent month's YTD results.
